title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a weekend walk.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Are you still coming on the walk on Saturday? The weather may be wet in the morning. || Man: I checked again. It should be dry after lunch, but the forest path will be muddy. || Woman: So should we change the plan and walk along the river instead? || Man: Yes. [8]The river path is flatter and it has cafés nearby, so it will be better if the ground is wet[/8]. We can still meet at the station at one.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Are you still coming on the walk on Saturday? The weather may be wet in the morning.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I checked again. It should be dry after lunch, but the forest path will be muddy.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: So should we change the plan and walk along the river instead?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes. The river path is flatter and it has cafés nearby, so it will be better if the ground is wet. We can still meet at the station at one.

questions.question: What do they decide to do?
questions.explanation: The man says the river path is flatter and better if the ground is wet, so they decide to walk by the river.

extracts.context: You hear a boy talking to his sister about a birthday present.
extracts.audio_script: Boy: Mum's birthday is next week, and I still haven't bought anything. || Girl: What about flowers? She always likes them, but Dad may buy those already. || Boy: I thought about a book, but she has several she hasn't read yet. || Girl: [9]Let's get her a cooking lesson because she keeps saying she wants to learn new dishes[/9]. I can book it online tonight, and you can make the card. That should make the plan easier and help everyone know what to expect.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Mum's birthday is next week, and I still haven't bought anything.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: What about flowers? She always likes them, but Dad may buy those already.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: I thought about a book, but she has several she hasn't read yet.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Let's get her a cooking lesson because she keeps saying she wants to learn new dishes. I can book it online tonight, and you can make the card. That should make the plan easier and help everyone know what to expect.

questions.question: What present does the girl suggest?
questions.explanation: The girl says they should get their mother a cooking lesson because she wants to learn new dishes.

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about lunch at work.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I'm going out for lunch. Do you want anything from the sandwich shop? || Woman: Maybe, but the queue there is always terrible on Fridays. || Man: The café across the road is quicker, though it costs a bit more. || Woman: [10]I'd rather bring something from the supermarket and eat in the office garden[/10]. It's sunny, and I don't want to spend my whole break waiting. I think that is the most sensible choice after looking at the other possibilities.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I'm going out for lunch. Do you want anything from the sandwich shop?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Maybe, but the queue there is always terrible on Fridays.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: The café across the road is quicker, though it costs a bit more.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I'd rather bring something from the supermarket and eat in the office garden. It's sunny, and I don't want to spend my whole break waiting. I think that is the most sensible choice after looking at the other possibilities.

questions.question: What does the woman prefer to do?
questions.explanation: The woman says she would rather buy something from the supermarket and eat in the office garden.

extracts.context: You hear a message about a swimming class.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: This is a message for everyone in Tuesday's swimming class. The main pool is closed for repairs this week. || Woman: Some people asked if the class would move to Friday, but that is not possible. || Woman: [11]Instead, the lesson will be in the small pool at the usual time of six thirty[/11]. Please arrive ten minutes early because there is a different entrance. It means there should be fewer problems when the time comes to do it.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: This is a message for everyone in Tuesday's swimming class. The main pool is closed for repairs this week.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Some people asked if the class would move to Friday, but that is not possible.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Instead, the lesson will be in the small pool at the usual time of six thirty. Please arrive ten minutes early because there is a different entrance. It means there should be fewer problems when the time comes to do it.

questions.question: What has changed about the swimming class?

options.B: what day it will be on

questions.explanation: The speaker says the lesson will be in the small pool, so the place has changed, not the day or time.

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a new phone.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Is that your new phone? It looks the same as your old one. || Man: It does, but the camera is much better. I nearly bought the cheaper model. || Woman: Was the battery different? || Man: Not really. [12]I chose this one because the screen is easier to read outdoors[/12]. I use maps a lot when I'm cycling, so that matters more than the camera. I would rather decide now than leave everything until the last minute.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Is that your new phone? It looks the same as your old one.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It does, but the camera is much better. I nearly bought the cheaper model.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Was the battery different?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Not really. I chose this one because the screen is easier to read outdoors. I use maps a lot when I'm cycling, so that matters more than the camera. I would rather decide now than leave everything until the last minute.

questions.question: Why did the man choose this phone?
questions.explanation: The man says he chose the phone because the screen is easier to read outdoors.

extracts.context: You hear a woman telling a friend about a hotel.
extracts.audio_script: Man: How was your hotel by the beach? The photos looked amazing. || Woman: The room was clean, and breakfast was good, but we had one problem. || Man: Was it too far from the sea? || Woman: No, that part was fine. [13]At night the music from the restaurant downstairs was really loud[/13], and we couldn't sleep until after midnight. That way, we can enjoy it more and avoid rushing around later. It is not perfect, but it seems better than the other choices we had.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: How was your hotel by the beach? The photos looked amazing.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: The room was clean, and breakfast was good, but we had one problem.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Was it too far from the sea?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: No, that part was fine. At night the music from the restaurant downstairs was really loud, and we couldn't sleep until after midnight. That way, we can enjoy it more and avoid rushing around later. It is not perfect, but it seems better than the other choices we had.

questions.question: What did the woman dislike about the hotel?
questions.explanation: The woman says the music from the restaurant downstairs was really loud at night.
